expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: canonical top-layer grouping without collapsing scenarios
expectations.evidence: In result.md, Phase 4b contract coverage is explicitly cited: “Group the Phase 4a draft into the canonical top-layer taxonomy without merging away scenario granularity.” It also lists a “Canonical Top Layer” taxonomy and quotes scenario-preservation/anti-compression rules such as “preserve scenario nodes...” and “grouping and refactor may not silently shrink coverage.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4b
expectations.evidence: result.md asserts alignment to phase4b by referencing phase4b-contract.md and phase4b-specific outputs/validators: “Phase 4b `--post` requires `drafts/qa_plan_phase4b_r<round>.md`” and validators like “validate_phase4b_category_layering” and “validate_scenario_granularity,” which are Phase 4b responsibilities.
